faqs.question: What pantry pests are most common in College Point homes?
faqs.answer: College Point residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, flour beetles, weevils, and grain beetles. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, rice, nuts, and pet food. Our technicians identify the specific pest species and their harborage areas to provide targeted treatment throughout your pantry and kitchen.

faqs.question: How much does pantry pest control cost in College Point?
faqs.answer: Our pantry pest control services in College Point range from $350 to $550, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the extent of treatment needed. This includes inspection, treatment of infested areas, removal recommendations, and preventative measures to keep pests from returning.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide pantry pest service in College Point?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day pantry pest control service throughout College Point, including areas near MacNeil Park, Malba, and along Whitestone Expressway. Our team understands that pantry pests contaminate food quickly, so we prioritize rapid response to eliminate the infestation and help you identify affected products.

faqs.question: Will I need to throw away all my pantry food after treatment?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily all food, but infested items must be discarded. Our technicians will help you identify which products are contaminated and which sealed items are safe to keep. We recommend storing dry goods in airtight containers after treatment and provide guidance on proper food storage practices to prevent future pantry pest problems in your College Point home.
